proven.claim: Proven: VERSION = "0.3.0" is a module-level constant (5 chars).
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 537
; Proven: VERSION = "0.3.0" is a module-level constant (5 chars).
; The interpolated banner is deterministic and always non-empty.
(declare-const version_length Int)
(declare-const banner_length Int)
; const VERSION = "0.3.0" → length 5, initialized before main() runs
(assert (= version_length 5))
; banner = "neurallog v" (11) + VERSION (5) + " — a logger that fixes your code" (32)
(assert (= banner_length (+ 11 (+ version_length 32))))
; NEGATE: banner is empty (length <= 0)
(assert (not (> banner_length 0)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — 11 + 5 + 32 = 48 > 0, proven by arithmetic

proven.claim: Proven: the first statement executes unconditionally when printHelp() is entered;
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 537
; Proven: the first statement executes unconditionally when printHelp() is entered;
; no guard or branch can suppress it.
(declare-const fn_entered Int)
(declare-const stmt_537_fired Int)
; Precondition: printHelp was called by main()
(assert (= fn_entered 1))
; Transition: no branch between function entry and line 537 → fires iff entered
(assert (= stmt_537_fired fn_entered))
; NEGATE: stmt_537 does not fire despite function being entered
(assert (not (= stmt_537_fired 1)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — no path through printHelp skips the first statement

violations.claim: Reachable violation: printHelp() is invoked from two call sites in main() with
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 537
; Reachable violation: printHelp() is invoked from two call sites in main() with
; different exit codes following each invocation. The function cannot control or signal
; which exit code applies, so help output is accompanied by exit(1) on unknown commands.
(declare-const call_site Int)        ; 1 = help-check path, 2 = unknown-command default path
(declare-const exit_code Int)
; Call site 1: args.length===0 || --help || -h → printHelp() → process.exit(0)
(assert (=> (= call_site 1) (= exit_code 0)))
; Call site 2: default: case in switch → printHelp() → process.exit(1)
(assert (=> (= call_site 2) (= exit_code 1)))
; Both call sites are reachable (user can hit either)
(assert (or (= call_site 1) (= call_site 2)))
; Is exit(1) reachable after seeing the help output?
(assert (= exit_code 1))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — unknown command prints help but exits 1; CI pipelines will see a failure
; even though the user received the same output as a normal help request
